key_theorem.statement: Quantum measurement outcomes are EML-∞ as functions of any hidden variable λ. No EML-finite function O(a,λ) exists that reproduces all quantum correlations.
key_theorem.proof_sketch: Assume O(a,λ) is EML-k for some finite k. Then the CHSH expression is a linear combination of EML-k functions integrated against a probability distribution ρ(λ) — the result is EML-k, bounded by |·| ≤ 2. But quantum mechanics achieves 2√2 > 2. Contradiction. Therefore O(a,λ) must be EML-∞ for at least some λ.

quantum_states.name: |0⟩
quantum_states.born_rule_reason: |c|² = exp(2·Re(ln c)) — two EML gates → EML-2

quantum_states.name: |+⟩
quantum_states.born_rule_reason: |c|² = exp(2·Re(ln c)) — two EML gates → EML-2

quantum_states.name: |-⟩
quantum_states.born_rule_reason: |c|² = exp(2·Re(ln c)) — two EML gates → EML-2

quantum_states.name: |+i⟩
quantum_states.born_rule_reason: |c|² = exp(2·Re(ln c)) — two EML gates → EML-2

coherent_state.reason: Coherent state amplitudes are EML-1 (Poisson = Boltzmann in disguise)

wigner_negativity.comment: Wigner negativity is a quantum signature. Classically W ≥ 0 (EML-1 Gaussian). Quantum excited states W_n < 0 → breaks classical probability interpretation. But W_n itself is EML-3 (not EML-∞): the quantum structure is expressible in EML-3.

bell_inequality.hidden_variable_theorem: Bell's theorem: no EML-finite hidden variable λ can reproduce quantum correlations. The measurement outcome distribution as a function of λ must be EML-∞.
bell_inequality.eml_consequence: Quantum randomness is EML-∞ in any hidden variable: if outcome O(a,λ) were EML-finite in λ, CHSH would be ≤ 2; but quantum mechanics achieves 2√2 > 2 → EML-∞ required.

bell_state_mutual_info.reason: S = -Σ p·ln p = EML-2 (x·ln x is EML-2)

kl_divergence_example.reason: D(ρ||σ) = Σ p·(ln p - ln q) = EML-2

born_rule_probability.reason: |c|² = exp(2 Re(ln c)); modulus squared = EML-2

quantum_amplitude_eml1.description: Quantum amplitude cₙ = exp(iθₙ)/√Z per basis state
quantum_amplitude_eml1.reason: Single exp gate; phase factor = EML-1 atom

wigner_vacuum.reason: exp(-2H/ħω) — Boltzmann factor at each phase space point

wigner_excited_n.description: Wigner W_n for excited states: Laguerre × Gaussian
wigner_excited_n.reason: L_n(x) = polynomial in x → EML-2; times exp → EML-3

tsirelson_bound.reason: √2 = exp(½ ln 2) = EML-2; classical bound 2 = EML-0

quantum_entropy_vn.description: Von Neumann entropy S(ρ) = -Tr(ρ ln ρ)
quantum_entropy_vn.reason: x·ln x = EML-2; same as Shannon entropy

quantum_mutual_info.reason: Linear combination of EML-2 entropies = EML-2

quantum_outcome_hidden_variable.description: Measurement outcome O(a,λ) as function of hidden variable λ
quantum_outcome_hidden_variable.reason: Bell's theorem: no EML-finite λ-function reproduces QM correlations
examples: Any λ-model for singlet correlations must be EML-∞ in λ

coherent_state_photon_dist.reason: Poisson = Boltzmann factor = EML-1 (same class as stat mech)

eml_depth_summary.EML-0: Topological quantum numbers, Chern numbers (TQFT partition functions)
eml_depth_summary.EML-1: Quantum amplitudes exp(iθ), Poisson photon statistics, vacuum Wigner function
eml_depth_summary.EML-2: Born rule probabilities |c|², quantum entropy, Tsirelson bound 2√2
eml_depth_summary.EML-3: Wigner function W_n (Laguerre×Gaussian), Bell correlator cos(θ), harmonic oscillator eigenstates
eml_depth_summary.EML-\u221e: Hidden variable functions violating Bell, quantum measurement outcomes in λ-space

connections.to_session_57: Coherent state photon distribution = Boltzmann/Poisson = EML-1 (stat mech connection)
connections.to_session_58: TQFT partition function = EML-0 (Chern-Simons) — topology connection
connections.to_session_61: Path integral amplitude = EML-1 per history (QFT connection)
connections.to_session_69: Quantum randomness = EML-∞ like algorithmic randomness; both transcend EML-finite
